data: Le iscrizioni greche e latine di Messina
Irma Bitto, Le iscrizioni greche e latine di Messina, vol. 1, Pelorias 7 (Messina: Dipartimento di Scienze dell’Antichità dell’Università degli Studi di Messina, 2001)
Fazello records hederae in lines 1 and 3, Gualtherus in line 1 only.

six lines of Greek, carefully laid out along guielines.
lunate letters, with extensive use of ligature and occasional small letters mid-line.

The text is centered on the stone, with a careful layout (ordinatio) and uniform distribution.
Griffo, 1876-, Notizie degli scavi di antichità
The text is approximately centered on the stone, with the left and right margins more or less regular except in the final two lines.
Ferrua suggested early 3rd century CE, Griffo the  1st century CE; a date in the first two centuries CE seems likely (so also Bitto 2001: 71).
Orsi, 1916, Messana. La necropoli romana di S. Placido e di altre scoperte avvenute nel 1910-1915
Manganaro, 1988, La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Manganaro, 1996, Asklepios e Hygieia 'divinita salvatrici e protettrici della citta' a Messina

De Salvo, 1979, A proposito di alcuni iscrizioni di naukleroi in Sicilia
Rizzo, 1989, La menzione del lavoro nelle epigrafi della Sicilia antica: per una storia della mentalità
De Salvo, 1992, Economia privata e pubblici servizi nell'impero romano. I. Corpora naviculariorum
Epitaph for a freedman, Ioulios Diadoumenos
epitaph for a freedman, ioulios diadoumenos
Epitaph for Felicita, daughter of Hermodorus
epitaph for felicita, daughter of hermodorus
Five lines of Greek, centred on the stone with an attempt to maintain regular margins. Guide lines are visible. Words are separated by substnatial interpuncts, and the final line is visibly smaller.
Remains of five lines of text, poorly fitted to the width of the stone, overlapping the right border repeatedly.
The text is carefully aligned on the left margin, less evenly along the right, and fills the stone over 5 lines, with a large hedera at the end of the text, lower right.
The text is centered on the stone, with a careful layout (ordinatio) and uniform distribution. The first line with the name of the deceased appears in a larger size.
Latin text laid out neatly over four lines, neatly filling the available space (regular left margin, irregular right margin). Line 1 has large hedera at each end.

Neatly v-cut letters, with faint traces of guidelines. The letters have deep serifs at the end of strokes: E is often very narrow, with the horizontal bars angled upwards; M and A in the final line have extended upper strokes, tending towards cursive; interpuncts are variable in form.
The text is centered on the stone but lacks a careful layout (ordinatio) and uniform distribution.
Text centred on the upper part of the plaque.
Four lines of Latin letters approximately centred on the stone

Thinly but deeply incised regular letters with minimal serifs and of slightly uneven size. R has a small open eye, with a long straight tail; E is fully perpendicular with equal length strokes; M is made up four equal oblique strokes reaching to top and bottom of the line.
Five lines of Greek text, observing a regular left margin, with hedera in the four corners

Somewhat irregular letters, tending to cursive (e.g. mu), and often leaning to the left; omega, sigma and epislon all lunate in form; theta a circle with an unconnected central bar; triangular letters tend to have a slightly extended top stroke.
Text is centred, with a fixed left margin and irregular right margin, with hedera set in the lower corners.
lunate epsilon, sigma and omega, ornate hederae.
Text is more or less centered and concentrated on the upper portion of the plaque, with extensive use of interpuncts. Perhaps a single letter missing from the end of line 2.
Diamond shaped theta, broken and straight-bar alpha, beta with detached loops, sigma a slightly irregular four-bar version, epsilon with upward angled cross-strokes.
Ends of two lines preserved, with a consistent right margin, with a hedera below on the right margin. Guidelines are clearly visible in the photograph of the cast.
Large letters that essentially fill the available surface of the stone in four lines.
Large deep letters, with slight tendency to extensions/serifs at stroke ends. Borken bar alpha (but also straight on line 2), lunate sigma, regular epsilon, use of ligature.
Text set out on centre of stone, below the tympanum (guideline marks the base of the tympanum).
Regular deeply v-cut letters with some curvature on longer strokes. Lunate sigma and epsilon; kappa has short arms. Elegant hedera at end of line 1. Phi in line 1 and Iota in line 3 are extended.
Text is centred on the upper part of the plaque, over five lines, with a concluding hedera.
Slightly irregularly spaced thinly cut lunate Greek letters: lunate epsilon and sigma, cursive omega, terminations to many of the strokes.
Text is centred with both margins justified, and filling the available surface.
somewhat crudely and irregularly cut letters of regular height. Lunate eipsilon and sigma, cursive omega and mu; extensive use of simple hederae for word division.
Text is centred, with a straight left margin and an irregular right margin.
V-cut lunate Greek letters, slightly irregular: lunate sigma and epsilon, cursive omega, regular mu with short middle strokes.
remains of two lines, leaving a clear margin of space on the left.
Text was apparently centred, but assuming a fourth line the left margin was not maintained but indented.
Lunate epsilon, crossed Z for xsi.
Remains of the right-hand end of four lines of Latin text. Line 1 may be centered, lines 2-4 do not maintain a regular margin.
first or second century CE (lettering, context)
Orsi (Comparetti), 1916, Messana. La necropoli romana di S. Placido e di altre scoperte avvenute nel 1910-1915
López Jimeno, 1991, Las tabellae defixionis de la Sicilia griega
